pattern: An index only needs to beat the average of everything after it, and 'greater than an average' is the same fact as 'greater than the average times the count' -- so a single right-to-left pass carrying a running sum and count turns a seemingly averaging problem into pure integer comparisons.
hints: Comparing a value against a running average invites floating point trouble -- there's a way to check nums[i] > average(rest) using only integer multiplication.
nums[i] > sum(rest) / len(rest) is equivalent to nums[i] * len(rest) > sum(rest), which avoids division entirely.

Walk the array from the last index to the first, maintaining the running sum and count of elements already seen (which are exactly the elements to the current index's right), so each index is checked in O(1) before being folded into the running totals.

edge_inputs.description: single element -- no elements to the right, zero dominant indices
all equal values -- strictly-greater must exclude ties with the average
strictly increasing values -- no index should ever beat the average of a larger suffix
constraint ceiling on length, all max value
average has a fractional part that a naive float compare could round the wrong way

stress.description: random arrays across the full length and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
